title: Human Genome Project - Complete Map
definition: The Human Genome Project was an international collaboration that successfully determined the DNA sequence of the entire human genome from 1990-2003. It produced the first reference genome, enabling modern genomics and personalized medicine.

proofs.description: Initial draft sequence published in Nature

difficulty_levels.beginner: The Human Genome Project was a large-scale research effort to understand the complete sequence of human DNA. Imagine it like a giant puzzle where scientists pieced together the genetic blueprint of humans.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The Human Genome Project was an international collaboration from 1990-2003 that determined the DNA sequence of the entire human genome, producing the first reference genome and enabling modern genomics research and personalized medicine. Its success involved a combination of genetic mapping, DNA sequencing methods, and bioinformatics.
difficulty_levels.expert: The Human Genome Project was a groundbreaking international collaboration from 1990-2003 that aimed to sequence the entire human genome. It represented a monumental leap in our understanding of genetics and its role in disease, evolution, and personalized medicine. The project's success involved significant advances in DNA sequencing technologies, genetic mapping, and bioinformatics.

extra_fields.historical_context: The Human Genome Project emerged from the rapidly advancing field of genomics and was a significant milestone in the history of science.
limitations: The project's focus on a single reference genome may not capture the genetic diversity within human populations. Additionally, ethical concerns around privacy and discrimination have been raised due to the increasing ability to analyze individual genomes.
applications: Personalized medicine (tailored treatments based on an individual's genetic makeup), Genome-wide association studies (GWAS) (identifying genes associated with diseases), Forensic science (identifying individuals from DNA evidence)
extra_fields.key_people: Francis Collins, Craig Venter, Eric Lander
